admin_app_handoff_empty: No app schemes configured. Add one to surface an "Open in App" CTA on the plugin detail page.
admin_app_handoff_kinds_hint: Comma-separated list of plugin kinds this scheme handles (e.g. "theme, widget"). Leave empty for any kind.
admin_app_handoff_subtitle: Register URL schemes for desktop apps that can install plugins from this registry. The plugin detail page shows an "Open in App" CTA whenever a matching scheme is configured.
admin_audit_subtitle: Last 200 admin actions on this instance.
admin_audit_total_subtitle: Older entries are retained until manually pruned via SQL.
admin_branding_analytics_note: Injected verbatim into <head>. Only paste trusted snippets (Plausible / Umami).
admin_branding_fallback_placeholder: Falls back to {locale} if left empty
admin_branding_footer_subtitle: Footer text is per-language; empty languages fall back to the default locale.
admin_branding_indexing_note: When off: robots.txt returns Disallow and pages get noindex meta.
admin_branding_logo_note: SVG or PNG. Renders next to the header name.
admin_branding_name_note: Not translated — your brand name stays consistent across languages.
admin_branding_subtitle: Whitelabel name, tagline, colors, logo, footer, analytics. Changes apply instantly.
admin_branding_tagline_subtitle: Shown in the hero of the default home page. Per-language — empty languages fall back to the default locale.
admin_features_requests_subtitle_middle: page hides the create form and
admin_features_requests_subtitle_suffix: returns 403. Existing requests stay listed and upvoting still works.
admin_features_submissions_subtitle_suff: returns 403. Existing plugins keep working — only new submissions are blocked.
admin_features_subtitle: Toggle public-facing capabilities. Disabled features hide their UI and return 403 from the API.
admin_home_eyebrow_card_subtitle: The small pill above the hero headline.
admin_home_features_card_subtitle: Three-column block beneath the featured plugins.
admin_home_subtitle: Toggle and rewrite the eyebrow badge and feature section on the public home page. Empty fields fall back to the built-in copy.
admin_i18n_at_least_one: At least one locale must be enabled
admin_i18n_default_locked: The default language can't be disabled — change it above first.
admin_i18n_default_must_be_enabled: Default locale must be one of the enabled locales
admin_i18n_default_subtitle: Used for visitors with no saved choice and as a fallback for missing translations.
admin_i18n_enabled_subtitle: Visitors only see languages that are enabled here. Existing translated CMS pages stay in the database — disabling just hides them.
admin_i18n_subtitle: Pick the default language and toggle which languages users can choose. Disabled languages hide from the public switcher and fall back to the default.
admin_infra_cache_off: off — no cache, no rate limit
admin_infra_cache_subtitle_html: Used for the /api/plugins/:slug/latest read cache, rate-limit counters, and short-lived OAuth state. off disables read-caching and breaks rate-limits — only safe in tests. memory is the right default for single-node deploys. redis is required for multi-node.
admin_infra_redis_url_note: Stored encrypted. Leave blank to reuse the currently configured URL. Works with Dragonfly too — same protocol.
admin_infra_storage_subtitle_html: Where uploaded files live (provider logos today; asset mirroring later). disk writes under data/uploads/ and serves via /uploads/. s3 via Bun.S3Client — works with AWS, MinIO, R2.
admin_infra_subtitle: Hot-swap cache, queue, storage and mail backends without a restart.
admin_instance_approval_subtitle_prefix: When on, new submissions land in
admin_instance_rate_limits_subtitle: Per-bucket limit (max requests) within the window (seconds). Subject = authenticated user when present, otherwise client IP.
admin_instance_require_approval: Require admin approval for new plugins
admin_kinds_delete_confirm: Delete this kind? Existing plugins keep the tag but stop appearing in the kind facet.
admin_kinds_duplicate: A kind with this key already exists
admin_kinds_empty: No kinds defined yet. Add one above.
admin_kinds_ext_empty_reverts: Empty means plugins of this kind fall back to the global extensions delta.
admin_kinds_ext_hint: Plugins with this kind validate against these properties instead of the global extensions. Same JSON Schema delta format.
admin_kinds_ext_toggle: Custom schema extensions for this kind
admin_kinds_field_key_hint: Lowercase, digits, dashes (e.g. "theme", "sql-template"). Immutable after creation.
admin_kinds_subtitle: Define the categories your end-user app uses to filter plugins. Plugin authors mark their plugin by adding the matching tag.
admin_manifest_allowed_files_hint: Plugin authors put one of these files at the repo root. The fetcher tries each in order. Defaults cover dotfile + plain forms in YAML and JSON.
admin_manifest_card_subtitle: Choose which manifest filenames plugin authors can use and where the schema is served.
admin_manifest_core_card_subtitle: Validated by the registry. Not editable.
admin_manifest_core_desc_category: Top-level grouping such as theme, integration, automation.
admin_manifest_core_desc_description: Short summary shown in listings and detail views.
admin_manifest_core_desc_homepage: Public homepage URL for the plugin.
admin_manifest_core_desc_icon: URL or relative path to the plugin icon.
admin_manifest_core_desc_kind: Plugin kind — drives per-kind extension validation.
admin_manifest_core_desc_license: SPDX identifier or short license name.
admin_manifest_core_desc_name: Plugin slug — lowercase, unique within the registry.
admin_manifest_core_desc_readme: Path or URL to long-form README content.
admin_manifest_core_desc_screenshots: Array of image URLs displayed in the detail view.
admin_manifest_core_desc_support: URL or contact info for issues and questions.
admin_manifest_core_desc_tags: Free-form labels for search and filtering.
admin_manifest_core_locked_hint: Plugin authors can also include $schema (an IDE hint) — it's stripped before persistence.
admin_manifest_ext_advanced_toggle: Edit this property as raw JSON Schema
admin_manifest_ext_card_subtitle: Adds first-class fields on top of the core. The merged schema is served at /manifest.schema.json so IDEs autocomplete everything.
admin_manifest_ext_description_placehold: Shown in IDE tooltips when authors hover the field.
admin_manifest_ext_empty: No extensions yet. Add one to start tailoring the manifest to your app.
admin_manifest_ext_empty_body: Add fields plugin authors should declare. Start blank, paste an existing JSON Schema, or pick a template to see the shape.
admin_manifest_ext_empty_title: Tailor the manifest to your app
admin_manifest_ext_json_hint: Flat record of property names mapping to JSON Schema nodes. Server validates type whitelist, depth, $ref absence, and that no key shadows a core field.
admin_manifest_ext_json_invalid: Invalid JSON — fix syntax before switching views
admin_manifest_ext_json_label: Full extensions delta as JSON Schema
admin_manifest_filename_invalid: Invalid filename — letters, digits, dashes; optional dot prefix; optional .yaml/.yml/.json extension.
admin_manifest_filename_one_required: At least one filename must remain
admin_manifest_page_subtitle: Extend the locked Tabularium core with extra fields your app wants plugin authors to declare.
admin_manifest_preview_subtitle: Merged schema as IDEs see it.
admin_manifest_preview_url_hint: Point plugin authors' $schema at this URL for IDE autocomplete.
admin_manifest_schema_url_hint: Public URL where the manifest spec is served. Defaults to BASE_URL/manifest.schema.json.
admin_page_edit_help_html: Raw HTML — sanitized server-side (whitelist of structural tags + <tabularium-widget />). Use Tailwind utility classes for layout.
admin_page_edit_help_markdown: GFM supported. Inline HTML allowed (sanitized server-side). Switch to HTML mode for full structural control.
admin_page_edit_path_note_prefix: = homepage override. Otherwise any non-reserved URL (eg.
admin_pages_empty: No pages yet. Create your first below.
admin_pages_internal_slug_note: Used as the row identifier. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens.
admin_pages_new_subtitle: Page is created as a draft. Edit it after creation to set content + publish.
admin_pages_subtitle_prefix: Markdown-driven content. Rendered server-side with sanitization. Reachable at
admin_plugins_bulk_delete_confirm: Delete {count} plugin(s) and their releases? This is permanent.
admin_plugins_card_subtitle_middle: from the webhook ingest until approved. Toggle approval mode in
admin_plugins_rejection_prompt: Reason for rejection (shown to owner via webhook):
admin_plugins_subtitle: Browse, approve, reject, or delete plugins.
admin_providers_add_subtitle_prefix: Register OAuth client credentials. The callback URL to enter on the provider side is
admin_providers_configured_subtitle: One row per OAuth client. Disable to hide from the login page without losing the credentials.
admin_providers_empty: No instances yet. Add your first one below.
admin_providers_instance_id_note: Lowercase, hyphens. Becomes the OAuth route.
admin_providers_logo_note: Defaults to the simpleicons CDN brand mark.
admin_providers_subtitle: Configure OAuth clients. End users sign in with any enabled instance.
admin_recovery_card_subtitle: Keeps the bootstrap email/password login available as an emergency unlock if you accidentally remove your only OAuth provider. Bootstrap is permanently closed after first OAuth link by default; flip this on (while you can still sign in) to preserve it.
admin_recovery_delete_confirm: Remove the bootstrap email/password? You can re-add them later from this page.
admin_recovery_field_validation: Email and a password of at least 8 characters are required
admin_recovery_no_credential: No recovery credentials are set for your admin account yet.
admin_recovery_persist_hint: When off, root credentials are deleted on the first OAuth link (default, more secure). When on, they stay so you can sign in at /login/admin even if every OAuth provider is removed.
admin_recovery_persist_label: Keep email recovery available after OAuth is linked
admin_security_backfill_card_subtitle: Re-hash legacy releases (those ingested before the integrity table existed) so they expose an integrity field. Safe to re-run — idempotent on (release_id, name).
admin_security_backfill_started: Backfill started. Watch the audit log for completion.
admin_security_budget_card_subtitle: Per-asset budget for ingest-side SHA-256 hashing. The registry does not host assets — this caps how big an asset URL the ingest pipeline is willing to stream-download to compute its hash. Assets above the budget still ingest but appear without an integrity entry; you'll see an audit warning.
admin_security_key_card_subtitle: The Ed25519 key the registry uses to sign release-integrity payloads. Consumers verify against the JWKS at .well-known/registry-key.json.
admin_security_rotate_confirm: Rotate the registry signing key? The current key moves to the previous slot — signatures it produced will still verify until the next rotation. New releases will be signed with the new key.
admin_security_rotate_success: Signing key rotated. Previous key moved to verification-only slot.
admin_security_subtitle: Release signing key, asset-hash budget, and integrity backfill.
admin_users_subtitle: Promote, demote, edit display name. Last-admin and self-demote are blocked server-side.
error_404_body: That page doesn't exist or has been removed.
error_generic_body: An unexpected error occurred. Try again in a moment.
home_feature_dropin_body: Point your client at the registry and fetch the plugin index directly.
home_feature_providers_body: Admin configures any number of provider instances. Users submit via OAuth on any of them.
home_feature_release_body: Wire a webhook once. New tags automatically refresh the version table, no PRs required.
home_latest_subtitle: Most recently updated on this instance.
init_bootstrap_card_subtitle: These credentials work only until setup is complete.
init_database_subtitle: Postgres, MySQL, or SQLite. URL prefilled from env if set.
init_done_body_prefix: The server is restarting. Sign in at
init_done_body_suffix: and the password you just used.
init_login_subtitle: Sign in with the temporary credentials printed in the server logs.
init_waiting_subtitle: Waiting for the server to come back. We'll log you in and open the admin panel automatically.
init_wizard_subtitle: Configure the database. Your bootstrap account becomes the permanent admin. The server restarts when you submit.
login_admin_bootstrap_subtitle: Only the initial admin account can sign in here, and only until they link any OAuth identity. Once linked, this path closes permanently and the admin uses OAuth like everyone else.
login_admin_card_subtitle: Email + password set during first-time setup.
login_admin_closed_body: The bootstrap admin has linked an OAuth account, so email + password recovery is no longer accepted. Sign in via your OAuth provider on the main login page.
login_admin_disable_hint_prefix: Want to disable this? Link an OAuth account from
login_no_providers: No sign-in providers are configured on this instance yet.
login_subtitle: Sign in to submit plugins and upvote requests.
login_token_note: Tokens are encrypted at rest. We only see your username and an access token used to verify repository ownership when you submit a plugin.
plugin_detail_download_empty: No platform artifacts attached to this release yet. Attach files via the Codeberg release UI or run fj release asset upload.
plugin_detail_install_subtitle: Resolve the latest platform-matching asset URL via the catalog API:
plugin_detail_open_in_app_subtitle: Hand off to the desktop app — it verifies the registry signature, downloads, and installs in one click.
plugin_detail_readme_missing: No README yet — wait for the next release webhook or refresh the manifest from /admin.
plugin_detail_releases_subtitle: Latest version is highlighted in the badge above. SHA256 column shows when integrity has been hashed.
plugin_detail_transfer_offered: Transfer offered — recipient must accept within 7 days
plugin_detail_transfer_prompt:
Offer this plugin to which user?

Enter the recipient's ULID (visible in their /settings page).
They have 7 days to accept — ownership only changes once they confirm.
plugins_list_empty: No plugins yet on this instance.
plugins_list_search_placeholder: Search by name, slug, or description…
requests_admin_delete_confirm: Delete this request? This is irreversible.
requests_disabled: New requests are currently disabled. You can still upvote existing entries below.
requests_empty: No requests yet — be the first to add one.
requests_subtitle: Community wishlist. Upvote to signal demand or add a new request.
settings_confirm_delete_account: Permanently delete your account? This unlinks all identities. Plugins you own must be transferred or deleted first.
settings_linked_identities_subtitle: OAuth accounts that can manage your plugins.
settings_pending_transfers_subtitle: Plugins waiting for your response or that you've offered to others.
submit_disabled: New plugin submissions are currently disabled on this instance.
submit_filter_placeholder: Filter repos by name or description…
submit_no_identities: No linked identities yet. Link one in settings.
submit_subtitle: Pick a repo you own and {name} will install the release webhook for you.
submit_switch_identity_hint: Switch identity to see other repos.
submit_webhook_installed_body: Releases will appear here within seconds of being published.
submit_webhook_needed_body: We couldn't install the webhook automatically. Add it manually with this secret:
welcome_intro_prefix: Let's get your registry set up. You can change everything later in
welcome_next_steps_middle: to wire Redis if you plan to scale, and
welcome_step1_subtitle: Give your registry a name and an accent color. Settings apply instantly.
welcome_step2_subtitle: Users need at least one provider to sign in. You can add more later. Skip to do it after — but submitting plugins will be disabled until at least one is configured.
welcome_step3_done: Provider added. Users can now sign in and submit plugins.
welcome_step3_skipped_prefix: You skipped provider setup — head to
welcome_step3_skipped_suffix: to add one before submitting plugins.
